quick_skim.title: A control-validated pan-proteome deep-learning pipeline nominates GPR35 as a candidate target of the orphan bacterial metabolite ligiamycin A
quick_skim.abstract: Most microbial natural products with documented bioactivity lack an identified molecular target, which limits their development. We present an open, control-validated computational pipeline for natural-product target hypothesis generation. It combines a pan-proteome deep-learning drug-target interaction (DTI) model (a graph neural-network ligand encoder, an ESM-2 protein language-model encoder, and bidirectional cross-attention) with bias-corrected ranking and control-anchored molecular docking. Applying it to ligiamycin A, a 2022-described Streptomyces/Achromobacter co-culture decalin-amino-maleimide with no reported target, we find that the predicted interactions of the compound are dominated by class-A G-protein-coupled receptors. Using a drug with a known target (losartan) we identify and correct a frequent-hitter bias in the raw model; after correction the standout candidates are uniformly class-A GPCRs, led by the orphan receptor GPR35. Structure-based docking with matched positive and negative controls across three candidates corroborates GPR35 specifically: ligiamycin A scores comparably to the known GPR35 agonist zaprinast at the agonist pocket (-8.1 vs -8.3 kcal/mol; non-binder floor -5.5), whereas FFAR1 is excluded and histamine H2 is inconclusive. We propose GPR35 as a prioritized, experimentally testable target and release the workflow as a reusable tool. The result is a computational hypothesis that requires experimental validation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: GNN and protein language model for drug-target interaction, transferable to DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图神经网络和蛋白质语言模型用于药物-靶标相互作用预测，可迁移至DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图神经网络和蛋白质语言模型用于药物-靶标相互作用预测，可迁移至DDI
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图神经网络和蛋白质语言模型用于药物-靶标相互作用预测，可迁移至DDI
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a DTI pipeline with GNN and ESM-2 protein language model; methodologically bridges to DDI prediction with LLMs and graphs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对天然产物靶标预测问题，提出一个结合图神经网络与蛋白质语言模型（ESM-2）的深度学习管线，通过双向交叉注意力融合分子与蛋白质特征，并引入偏差校正排序和分子对接验证。在ligiamycin A靶标发现中成功预测候选靶点。其方法框架可迁移至药物-药物相互作用预测，尤其是结合生物医学语言模型与图结构的学习范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对天然产物靶标预测问题，提出一个结合图神经网络与蛋白质语言模型（ESM-2）的深度学习管线，通过双向交叉注意力融合分子与蛋白质特征，并引入偏差校正排序和分子对接验证。在ligiamycin A靶标发现中成功预测候选靶点。其方法框架可迁移至药物-药物相互作用预测，尤其是结合生物医学语言模型与图结构的学习范式。

quick_skim.title: KARMA: Knowledge graph-based Automated Reasoning Materialization and Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Template-based contrastive synthesis is scalable, but its candidates often differ only in a few entity-slots while sequence-level optimization spreads supervision over mostly shared templates. We formalize this as the Resolution Mismatch Problem and propose KARMA, which enumerates schema-constrained paths over domain knowledge graphs and verbalizes them into slot-aligned contrastive candidates. Slot-Parallel Alignment (SPA) then applies a decoupled slot-level objective to route preference supervision to discriminative entity-slots, with slot-aware masked attention serving as an optional packed-evaluation implementation. Across biomedical, computer-science, and chemistry benchmarks, KARMA outperforms base LLM and same-data SFT baselines, and compares favorably with sequence and token-level preference methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses knowledge graph paths for contrastive synthesis, transferable to drug interaction prediction with KG
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用知识图谱路径生成对比样本，可迁移至基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用知识图谱路径生成对比样本，可迁移至基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用知识图谱路径生成对比样本，可迁移至基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: KARMA proposes a knowledge-graph-based method for generating slot-aligned contrastive candidates using schema-constrained paths, improving LLM alignment; its KG-path enumeration is transferable to DDI prediction with KGs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对模板对比合成中的分辨率不匹配问题，提出KARMA方法，通过枚举领域知识图谱的约束路径并转化为插槽对齐的对比候选，结合解耦插槽级目标函数，引导LLM偏好学习。在生物医学等基准上优于基线和SFT。该方法的知识图谱路径枚举和插槽对齐技术可迁移至冷启动药物相互作用预测中的知识图谱增强任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对模板对比合成中的分辨率不匹配问题，提出KARMA方法，通过枚举领域知识图谱的约束路径并转化为插槽对齐的对比候选，结合解耦插槽级目标函数，引导LLM偏好学习。在生物医学等基准上优于基线和SFT。该方法的知识图谱路径枚举和插槽对齐技术可迁移至冷启动药物相互作用预测中的知识图谱增强任务。

quick_skim.title: RSF-GLLM: Bridging the Semantic Gap in Multi-Hop Knowledge Graph QA via Recurrent Soft-Flow and Decoupled LLM Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-hop Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs faces a critical challenge: traditional retrieve-then-read pipelines break differentiability, preventing the retriever from learning to bridge the semantic gap where intermediate nodes lack lexical overlap with the query. To address this, we propose RSF-GLLM, a framework decoupling differentiable graph reasoning from answer generation. Our Recurrent Soft-Flow (RSF) module employs a GRU-guided query updater to propagate continuous relevance scores, utilizing a dynamic gating mechanism to traverse semantically dissimilar bridge nodes via structural cues. We introduce flow sparsity regularization to theoretically guarantee convergence from soft probabilities to discrete reasoning paths. These paths are extracted and textualized to fine-tune a Large Language Model (LLM), ensuring generation is grounded in factual topology. Experiments on WebQSP and CWQ demonstrate that RSF-GLLM achieves competitive performance with superior inference efficiency compared to LLM based computationally expensive approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Knowledge graph reasoning with LLM generation, transferable to KG-enhanced DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 知识图谱推理与LLM生成相结合，可迁移至知识图谱增强的DDI预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 知识图谱推理与LLM生成相结合，可迁移至知识图谱增强的DDI预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 知识图谱推理与LLM生成相结合，可迁移至知识图谱增强的DDI预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RSF-GLLM for multi-hop KGQA with differentiable graph reasoning and LLM generation; method applicable to KG-based drug interaction prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多跳知识图谱问答中检索与生成不可微的问题，提出RSF-GLLM框架，通过GRU引导的查询更新器进行可微图推理，并解耦LLM生成。在合成数据集上验证了语义桥接能力。该方法将图神经网络与LLM结合，可直接用于构建基于知识图谱的药物-药物相互作用预测模型，尤其是冷启动场景下的关系推理。
